---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(xgboost)
library(randomForest)
library(glmnet)
library(e1071)
library(keras)
library(tidyr)
library(tensorflow)
library(lightgbm)
```


```{r}
setwd("C:\\Users\\aryan\\Documents\\Rutgers\\Sem 2\\Finanicial Data Mining\\Project\\CMaps")
```

```{r}
file_ids <- c("FD001", "FD002", "FD003", "FD004")
file_types <- c("train", "test", "RUL")

for (file_type in file_types) {
  for (file_id in file_ids) {
    txt_file <- paste0(file_type, "_", file_id, ".txt")
    csv_file <- paste0(file_type, "_", file_id, ".csv")
    
    data <- read.table(txt_file, header = FALSE, sep = " ")
    write.csv(data, csv_file, row.names = FALSE)
  }
}

```

```{r}
train_data_001 <- read.csv("train_FD001.csv", header = TRUE)
test_data_001 <- read.csv("test_FD001.csv", header = TRUE)

columns <- c('unit_number', 'time_in_cycles', 'setting_1', 'setting_2', 'TRA', 'T2', 'T24', 'T30', 'T50',
             'P2', 'P15', 'P30', 'Nf', 'Nc', 'epr', 'Ps30', 'phi', 'NRf', 'NRc', 'BPR', 'farB', 'htBleed',
             'Nf_dmd', 'PCNfR_dmd', 'W31', 'W32')
colnames(train_data_001) <- columns
colnames(test_data_001) <- columns

head(train_data_001)
```

```{r}
summary(train_data_001)
```

```{r}
# Removing the columns that are constant or close to constant
train_data_001 <- train_data_001[ ,-c(5,6,10,11,13,14,15,18,21,23,24,27,28)]
head(train_data_001)
summary(train_data_001)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
prepare_train_data <- function(data, factor = 0) {
  fd_RUL <- data %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    summarize(max_time = max(time_in_cycles), .groups = 'drop') # Calculate max per unit_number
  
  data <- merge(data, fd_RUL, by = "unit_number", all.x = TRUE)
  data$RUL <- data$max_time - data$time_in_cycles
  data <- subset(data, select = -max_time)
  data <- data[data$time_in_cycles > factor, ]
  
  return(data)
}

train_fd001 <- prepare_train_data(train_data_001)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)  

df_cor <- cor(train_fd001)  

# Convert the correlation matrix into a long format
melted_cor <- melt(df_cor)

ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +  # Add linewidths
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", high = "green", mid = "yellow",
                       midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1), space = "Lab",
                       name = "Correlation") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12)
  ) +
  coord_fixed()
```

```{r}
library(tidyr)
unit1_data <- train_fd001 %>% 
  filter(unit_number == 1)

# Plot sensor trends over engine cycles for a single unit
plot_sensor_trends <- function(data, unit_num) {
  unit_data <- data %>% filter(unit_number == unit_num)
  
  # Select only sensor columns
  sensor_cols <- setdiff(names(unit_data), c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles", "RUL", "setting_1", "setting_2"))
  
  # Reshape data for plotting
  unit_data_long <- unit_data %>% pivot_longer(cols = all_of(sensor_cols), 
                 names_to = "Sensor", 
                 values_to = "Value")
  
  # Create plots
  ggplot(unit_data_long, aes(x = time_in_cycles, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(aes(color = Sensor), linewidth = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~ Sensor, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(title = paste("Sensor Trends for Unit", unit_num),
         x = "Time (Cycles)",
         y = "Sensor Value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

# Plot sensor trends for unit 1
plot_sensor_trends(train_fd001, 1)
```

```{r}
unit_cycles <- train_fd001 %>%
  group_by(unit_number) %>%
  summarize(max_cycles = max(time_in_cycles)) %>%
  arrange(max_cycles)

# Select units from different parts of the distribution
short_life_unit <- unit_cycles$unit_number[1]
medium_life_unit <- unit_cycles$unit_number[round(nrow(unit_cycles)/2)]
long_life_unit <- unit_cycles$unit_number[nrow(unit_cycles)]

# Create dataset with these selected units
selected_units <- train_fd001 %>%
  filter(unit_number %in% c(short_life_unit, medium_life_unit, long_life_unit))

# Plot RUL over time for selected units
ggplot(selected_units, aes(x = time_in_cycles, y = RUL, color = as.factor(unit_number))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Full Life Cycles - Different Engine Units",
       x = "Time (Cycles)",
       y = "Remaining Useful Life (RUL)",
       color = "Unit Number") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  annotate("text", 
           x = max(selected_units$time_in_cycles) * 0.8, 
           y = max(selected_units$RUL) * 0.8,
           label = paste("Short life unit:", short_life_unit,
                        "\nMedium life unit:", medium_life_unit,
                        "\nLong life unit:", long_life_unit),
           size = 4)

```

```{r}
truncate_lifecycle <- function(data, unit_num, percentage) {
  unit_data <- data %>% filter(unit_number == unit_num)
  max_cycle <- max(unit_data$time_in_cycles)
  truncated_cycle <- round(max_cycle * percentage)
  
  truncated_data <- unit_data %>% 
    filter(time_in_cycles <= truncated_cycle)
  
  return(truncated_data)
}

# Select a unit for demonstration
demo_unit <- 3
full_lifecycle <- train_fd001 %>% filter(unit_number == demo_unit)
truncated_25 <- truncate_lifecycle(train_fd001, demo_unit, 0.25)
truncated_50 <- truncate_lifecycle(train_fd001, demo_unit, 0.50)
truncated_75 <- truncate_lifecycle(train_fd001, demo_unit, 0.75)

# Combine datasets with a label
truncated_data <- bind_rows(
  mutate(full_lifecycle, Truncation = "100% (Full)"),
  mutate(truncated_75, Truncation = "75%"),
  mutate(truncated_50, Truncation = "50%"),
  mutate(truncated_25, Truncation = "25%")
)

# Plot truncated lifecycles
ggplot(truncated_data, aes(x = time_in_cycles, y = RUL, color = Truncation)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = paste("Truncated Life Cycles - Unit", demo_unit),
       x = "Time (Cycles)",
       y = "Remaining Useful Life (RUL)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2")
```


```{r}
cor_with_rul <- df_cor["RUL", ]
cor_with_rul <- sort(cor_with_rul[!names(cor_with_rul) %in% c("RUL", "unit_number", "time_in_cycles")])
top_sensors <- names(tail(cor_with_rul, 5))
bottom_sensors <- names(head(cor_with_rul, 5))

# Plot top correlated sensors for a few units
key_sensors <- train_fd001 %>%
  filter(unit_number %in% c(1, 5, 10)) %>%
  select(unit_number, time_in_cycles, RUL, all_of(top_sensors)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(top_sensors), 
               names_to = "Sensor", 
               values_to = "Value")

ggplot(key_sensors, aes(x = time_in_cycles, y = Value, color = Sensor)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  facet_grid(Sensor ~ unit_number, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Top Sensors Correlated with RUL",
       x = "Time (Cycles)",
       y = "Sensor Value") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

```{r}
scoring_function <- function(actual, predicted) {
  diff <- predicted - actual
  score <- numeric(length(diff))
  
  score[diff < 0] <- exp(-diff[diff < 0]/13) - 1
  
  score[diff >= 0] <- exp(diff[diff >= 0]/10) - 1
  
  return(mean(score))
}
```

```{r}
engineer_features <- function(data, window_sizes = c(5, 10, 15)) {
  result <- data
  
  sensor_cols <- setdiff(names(data), c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles", "RUL", "setting_1", "setting_2"))
  
  units <- unique(data$unit_number)
  
  feature_dfs <- list()
  
  for (unit in units) {
    unit_data <- data[data$unit_number == unit, ]
    unit_features <- unit_data
    
    for (sensor in sensor_cols) {
      for (window in window_sizes) {
        if (nrow(unit_data) >= window) {
          col_name <- paste0(sensor, "_rolling_avg_", window)
          unit_features[[col_name]] <- c(rep(NA, window-1), 
                                         stats::filter(unit_data[[sensor]], rep(1/window, window), sides = 1)[(window):length(unit_data[[sensor]])])
        }
      }
      
      # Calculate differences (rate of change)
      col_name <- paste0(sensor, "_diff")
      unit_features[[col_name]] <- c(NA, diff(unit_data[[sensor]]))
      
      # Calculate percent change
      col_name <- paste0(sensor, "_pct_change")
      unit_features[[col_name]] <- c(NA, diff(unit_data[[sensor]]) / unit_data[[sensor]][-nrow(unit_data)] * 100)
      
      # Calculate moving standard deviation (volatility)
      for (window in window_sizes) {
        if (nrow(unit_data) >= window) {
          col_name <- paste0(sensor, "_rolling_std_", window)
          roll_sd <- sapply(1:(nrow(unit_data) - window + 1), function(i) {
            sd(unit_data[[sensor]][i:(i+window-1)])
          })
          unit_features[[col_name]] <- c(rep(NA, window-1), roll_sd)
        }
      }
    }
    
    # Time-based features
    # Normalized cycle (0 to 1 within each unit's lifecycle)
    unit_features$norm_cycle <- unit_data$time_in_cycles / max(unit_data$time_in_cycles)
    
    # Add to list of processed units
    feature_dfs[[length(feature_dfs) + 1]] <- unit_features
  }
  
  # Combine all units back together
  result <- do.call(rbind, feature_dfs)
  
  # Remove NA values (from rolling calculations)
  # For real applications, consider more sophisticated approaches than simple removal
  result <- result[complete.cases(result), ]
  
  return(result)
}

# Apply feature engineering
engineered_data <- engineer_features(train_fd001)
```

```{r}
# Function for k-fold cross-validation
kfold_cv <- function(data, k = 5, seed = 42) {
  set.seed(seed)
  units <- unique(data$unit_number)
  folds <- createFolds(units, k = k, list = TRUE, returnTrain = FALSE)
  
  fold_data <- list()
  for (i in 1:k) {
    test_units <- units[folds[[i]]]
    train_units <- setdiff(units, test_units)
    
    train_data <- data[data$unit_number %in% train_units, ]
    test_data <- data[data$unit_number %in% test_units, ]
    
    fold_data[[i]] <- list(train = train_data, test = test_data)
  }
  
  return(fold_data)
}

# Function for leave-one-unit-out cross-validation (LOUO)
louo_cv <- function(data) {
  units <- unique(data$unit_number)
  folds <- length(units)
  
  fold_data <- list()
  for (i in 1:folds) {
    test_unit <- units[i]
    train_units <- setdiff(units, test_unit)
    
    train_data <- data[data$unit_number %in% train_units, ]
    test_data <- data[data$unit_number %in% test_unit, ]
    
    fold_data[[i]] <- list(train = train_data, test = test_data)
  }
  
  return(fold_data)
}

# Apply k-fold CV
cv_folds <- kfold_cv(engineered_data, k = 5)

```

```{r}
# Prepare X and y for machine learning models
prepare_ml_data <- function(data) {
  # Remove non-feature columns
  X <- data %>% select(-c(unit_number, time_in_cycles, RUL))
  y <- data$RUL
  
  # Convert to matrix/vector for some models
  X_matrix <- as.matrix(X)
  
  return(list(X = X, X_matrix = X_matrix, y = y))
}

# Utility function to evaluate and report metrics
evaluate_model <- function(actual, predicted, model_name) {
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predicted - actual)^2))
  mae <- mean(abs(predicted - actual))
  score <- scoring_function(actual, predicted)
  
  cat(paste0("\n", model_name, " Performance:\n"))
  cat(paste0("RMSE: ", round(rmse, 4), "\n"))
  cat(paste0("MAE: ", round(mae, 4), "\n"))
  cat(paste0("PHM08 Score: ", round(score, 4), "\n"))
  
  return(data.frame(model = model_name, rmse = rmse, mae = mae, score = score))
}

# Function to calculate performance across all folds
cross_validate_model <- function(model_fn, folds, model_name) {
  results <- data.frame()
  fold_metrics <- list()
  
  for (i in 1:length(folds)) {
    cat(paste0("\nFold ", i, " for ", model_name, "\n"))
    
    # Prepare data
    train_data <- prepare_ml_data(folds[[i]]$train)
    test_data <- prepare_ml_data(folds[[i]]$test)
    
    # Train and predict
    model <- model_fn(train_data$X_matrix, train_data$y)
    predictions <- predict(model, test_data$X_matrix)
    
    # Evaluate
    fold_metrics[[i]] <- evaluate_model(test_data$y, predictions, paste0(model_name, " Fold ", i))
  }
  
  # Combine results
  all_metrics <- do.call(rbind, fold_metrics)
  
  # Calculate mean metrics
  mean_metrics <- all_metrics %>%
    summarize(
      model = first(model),
      mean_rmse = mean(rmse),
      mean_mae = mean(mae),
      mean_score = mean(score)
    )
  
  return(list(fold_metrics = all_metrics, summary = mean_metrics))
}
```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```
